name: Salt minimal medium (MM) with ibuprofen (IBU) as sole carbon source (PMC11892951)
description: Salt minimal medium (MM) with ibuprofen (IBU) as sole carbon source — medium reported in PMC11892951 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC11892951 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Salt minimal medium (MM) of the Santero/Seville lab; a phosphate-buffered mineral salts medium derived from the mineral medium of Dorn et al. (1974)' (In-text: 'salt minimal medium (MM) [43]'. Ref 43 = Hernaez MJ, Reineke W, Santero E. Genetic analysis of biodegradation of tetralin by a Sphingomonas strain. Appl Environ Microbiol. 1999;65(4):1806-1810. doi:10.1128/AEM.65.4.1806-1810.1999 (PMID 10103288; PMC91258). That paper in turn
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: The composition of the medium (in grams per liter ...) was as follows: Na2HPO4.2H2O, 3.48; KH2PO4 1; ... 'The medium ... was based on the medium described by Dorn et al. (1974).'
provenance.recipe_evidence: From PMC11892951 Methods (verbatim): 'Cultures in salt minimal medium (MM) [43] with IBU 1 g/L as carbon source were diluted one-fourth of the previous 20 ml saturated culture in aeration at 30 C for ~15 days...' and 'Commercial IBU sodium salt was purchased from Sigma-Aldrich.' Nitrogen source confirmed as ammonium in sibling Santero-lab papers on the same MM: 'minimal medium supplemented with nitrate 20 mM as electron acceptor and ammonium as nitrogen source' (Garcia-Romero et al., PMC4741004) and 'ammonia is the preferred nitrogen source and the strain does not grow with nitrate or urea' (I
provenance.base_medium: Salt minimal medium (MM) of the Santero/Canosa lab (Seville/Pablo de Olavide) = the carbon-free mineral (mineral salts) medium of Dorn, Hellwig, Reineke & Knackmuss, Arch Microbiol 1974;99:61-70, as used by ref 43 (Hernaez, Reineke, Santero, AEM 1999, PMC91258). Phosphate-buffered mineral salts with ammonium as N source and ammonium ferric citrate as iron source; IBU (Na salt) 1 g/L supplied as the sole carbon source.
provenance.base_medium_reference: In-text: 'salt minimal medium (MM) [43]'. Ref 43 = Hernaez MJ, Reineke W, Santero E. Genetic analysis of biodegradation of tetralin by a Sphingomonas strain. Appl Environ Microbiol. 1999;65(4):1806-1810. doi:10.1128/AEM.65.4.1806-1810.1999 (PMID 10103288; PMC91258). That paper in turn defines MM as the carbon-free mineral medium of Dorn E, Hellwig M, Reineke W, Knackmuss HJ. Isolation and characterization of a 3-chlorobenzoate degrading pseudomonad. Arch Microbiol. 1974;99:61-70. doi:10.1007/BF00696222. Numeric salt composition below is taken from a documented reproduction of the Dorn (1974) base medium: Mehboob F et al., Appl Environ Microbiol 2009;75(23):7567-7574, PMC2690828.

sources_chased.what: the paper itself (PMC11892951, corpus XML) - names the medium and carbon source
base-medium ref 43 = Hernaez, Reineke, Santero, AEM 1999 (PMC91258)
added: confirmed MM = carbon-free mineral medium of Dorn et al. 1974
MML rich medium = same mineral medium + 2 g/L tryptone + 1 g/L yeast extract

sources_chased.what: numeric Dorn et al. 1974 salt composition via a documented reproduction, Mehboob et al. AEM 2009 (PMC2690828)
trace-element / vitamin cocktail for the aerobic MM
sources_chased.how: Chased Hernaez 1999 (PMC91258), the TFA GEM paper (PMC7250832), and the Santero GSR paper (PMC9303464); none add a separate SL/Wolfe/Holliger trace or vitamin solution to the AEROBIC MM. The only mineral supplement stated in the Dorn base is ammonium ferric citrate (iron). The anaerobic reproduction (Mehboob 2009) adds Holliger 1993 vitamins+trace elements plus resazurin, but those are specific to the anaerobic build and are NOT applied here.

missing_before: disodium hydrogen phosphate dihydrate (Na2HPO4.2H2O, 3.48 g/L) - was listed as uncovered; confirmed as the main phosphate/buffer and Na source
ibuprofen sodium salt (1 g/L) - was listed as uncovered; confirmed as the sole carbon source
explicit concentrations for all salts (none were present in current_components)
recuration.notes: Base-medium identity is certain: MM = carbon-free mineral medium of Dorn et al. 1974 via ref 43 (Hernaez 1999). The salt concentrations are taken from a documented reproduction of that Dorn base (Mehboob 2009) rather than being restated in PMC11892951 or Hernaez 1999, hence medium confidence. Resazurin (0.005 g/L) in the Mehboob build is an anaerobiosis redox indicator, not a nutrient, and is omitted for this aerobic culture. No separate trace-metal or vitamin solution is documented for the aerobic MM - iron is supplied solely by ammonium iron(III) citrate; additional trace metals/vitamins are
